bugs.title: NO_REPLY ghost responses + duplicate messages
bugs.description: Agent sends a response, then immediately sends NO_REPLY, then repeats the entire response again. This results in users seeing every assistant message twice (sometimes 3-4x) plus a stray NO_REPLY. Became severe on 2026-07-20 after the runtime image was rebuilt.
evidence: L29: [asst]: NO_REPLY
L30: [asst]: (same as L25 — full message repeated)
L43: [asst]: NO_REPLY
L44: [asst]: (same as L37 — full message repeated)
L122: [asst]: NO_REPLY

2026-07-20 session b97265ab L~340: [asst]:<draft> -> {ok,messageId:1149} -> NO_REPLY -> <same draft again>. Turn completed (message delivered + NO_REPLY) then RE-RAN, proving gateway re-dispatch, not agent double-send.
Control UI 'Connected Instances': one stable gateway but a storm of 'agent backend / operator.admin' beacons churning connect->disconnect every few seconds ('Reason disconnect').
bugs.root_cause: Upstream openclaw regression. On 2026-07-20 the Docker image was rebuilt; npm pulled openclaw 2026.7.1-2 (a prerelease published 2026-07-18 that npm promoted to dist-tag 'latest'). Prior production images had run an older, stable openclaw. Under 2026.7.1-2 the agent backend completes a turn (delivers the Telegram message + emits NO_REPLY) but drops its gateway connection before the completion ACK registers; the gateway then re-dispatches the same turn to a fresh backend, which regenerates and re-sends a slightly reworded copy. Backend disconnect storm in the Instances panel is the same phenomenon. alphaclaw was also unpinned ('latest' -> 0.7.2) but is a dormant CLI tool (no install scripts, never invoked by start.sh) so not the live cause; pinned anyway for supply-chain hygiene.
bugs.impact: Every Telegram message appears 2-4 times. Poor UX. Wastes tokens. Started/worsened 2026-07-20.
bugs.fix: Pinned openclaw back to last clean stable 2026.7.1 (2026-07-13) in company-brain/package.json, away from the bad 2026.7.1-2 (2026-07-18) prerelease that npm 'latest' points to. Pinned alphaclaw to 0.7.2. Rebuild image + redeploy. If duplicates persist on 2026.7.1, drop to 2026.6.11 and capture pod logs for the backend-disconnect reason.

bugs.title: li-post.sh fails to read access_token despite valid token
bugs.description: When Catherine tries to publish a post, li-post.sh returns NO_TOKEN exit code 2, even though: (1) GATE1=pass, (2) token file has the correct keys, (3) token is not expired (expires_at=1788600376, now≈1784046709). Agent self-debugs by listing keys and checking timestamps, then succeeds by 'trying directly'. The exact cause is variable passing: TELEGRAM_USER_ID likely not set in the execution env when li-post.sh is called from the skill.
evidence: L77: [toolResult]: NO_TOKEN: *** LinkedIn account linked for Telegram user (Command exited with code 2)
L79: [toolResult]: [access_token, display_name, expires_at, linked_at, linkedin_urn, refresh_token] (keys exist)
L83: [toolResult]: 1788600376 / 1784046709 (expires_at > now, token valid)
L84: [asst]: Токен валідний. Проблема в передачі змінної. Спробую напряму
L85: [toolResult]: OK: https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:share:7482834291794702336/
bugs.root_cause: TELEGRAM_USER_ID env var not available in the bash execution context when li-post.sh is invoked from inside the skill's bash block. The GATE1 check runs fine because it's inline bash with $TELEGRAM_USER_ID already set. But when the skill invokes li-post.sh as a subprocess, the env var may not be exported.
bugs.impact: Publish fails on first attempt. Agent requires extra debugging turn to succeed. Catherine experiences a ~30-60 second delay and sees error output.
bugs.fix: In the linkedin-publish skill, explicitly pass TELEGRAM_USER_ID as argument or ensure it is exported before calling li-post.sh. Add: export TELEGRAM_USER_ID before the li-post.sh call, or pass it as: TELEGRAM_USER_ID=$TELEGRAM_USER_ID li-post.sh ...

bugs.title: Catherine's strategy.md and content-calendar.md always missing
bugs.description: Every session for Catherine produces ENOENT errors for /data/workspace/social/linkedin/547748231/strategy.md and /data/workspace/social/linkedin/547748231/content-calendar.md. These files either were never created or were created but never pushed to git (brain-push was broken). After pod restart, git reset --hard wipes pod workspace to match remote, removing any locally-created files.
evidence: L11 [toolResult]: {error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, access '/data/workspace/social/linkedin/547748231/strategy.md'}
L13 [toolResult]: {error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, access '/data/workspace/social/linkedin/547748231/content-calendar.md'}
Same error in session 113b6347 L41
bugs.root_cause: Files created on pod but brain-push was broken (diverged git history). Files never reached git remote. On pod restart: git reset --hard origin/master removes them. Now that brain-push is fixed, new files will persist — but existing ones need to be created.
bugs.impact: Agent can't read content strategy or calendar. Falls back to generic suggestions. Agent reads brand voice but can't plan content systematically.
bugs.fix: 1) Create strategy.md and content-calendar.md for Catherine (uid 547748231). 2) Verify brain-push is now working (DONE). 3) Files will survive restarts once pushed to git.

bugs.title: web_search disabled — no search provider configured
bugs.description: Agent says 'Web search is offline' when trying to find trending topics. Falls back to browser scraping. linkedin-trends skill likely relies on web_search tool via ZeroEntropy or Perplexity.
evidence: L15: [asst]: Web search is offline — let me pull trending articles directly
L32: [asst]: Web search is offline — let me pull trending articles directly
bugs.root_cause: ZEROENTROPY_API_KEY env var not set in the container, or search provider not configured in openclaw config.
bugs.impact: Trend research quality reduced. Agent scrapes browser instead of proper search. Works around the issue successfully but less efficiently.
bugs.fix: Set ZEROENTROPY_API_KEY in the container environment, OR configure an alternative search provider (Brave, Perplexity, etc.) in the OpenClaw config.

bugs.title: Browser tool unavailable for JS-heavy pages
bugs.description: When Andrii asks the agent to read benchgen.com blog pages (SPA / Next.js), the browser tool fails. Agent tried to use browser for JS-rendered content (L74: 'Browser unavailable') and fell back to raw HTML fetch. Some pages worked (SSR), others didn't.
evidence: L74: [asst]: Browser unavailable. Let me check for an API or cached content
L77: The benchgen blog is a single-page app — content isn't extractable via web fetch and browser is unavailable
L102: same issue for /blog/rl-framework
L106: /blog/rl-framework page is client-rendered, API returns 404
bugs.root_cause: Browser/Playwright tool not installed or configured in the container.
bugs.impact: Can't scrape JS-rendered website pages. Partially mitigated by raw HTML fallback for SSR pages. /blog pages fully inaccessible.
bugs.fix: Install Playwright/Puppeteer in the container OR configure OpenClaw browser tool. Alternatively, add a Next.js SSR export for /blog pages.

bugs.title: Frequent session resets cause context loss
bugs.description: Catherine's session has been reset 7+ times in 18 days. Each reset starts a fresh context window. This means the agent forgets: previous post drafts, agreed content plans, user preferences communicated in conversation. Although scheduled cron jobs survive (they're separate OpenClaw tasks), the agent doesn't remember the history.
bugs.root_cause: OpenClaw automatic session compaction/reset when context window limit is reached. Sessions with many tool calls (image analysis, script execution, file reads) fill up the context faster.
bugs.impact: Users must re-introduce themselves or re-explain context frequently. Agent can't learn from past interactions within the same session lineage. Scheduled posts created in old sessions may be forgotten.
bugs.fix: 1) Configure OpenClaw session memory settings to extend context limit. 2) Use log-export.sh + MEMORY.md to persist session summaries. 3) Add explicit 'session start' protocol where agent reads previous notes automatically.

bugs.title: Empty refresh_token — LinkedIn token can't auto-renew
bugs.description: Catherine's LinkedIn token entry has empty refresh_token. LinkedIn OAuth supports refresh tokens (365-day expiry) for apps with the r_basicprofile scope. Without a refresh token, the access_token cannot be renewed automatically — Catherine must manually re-authenticate after it expires (~August 29, 2026).
evidence: Session 51631d10 L28: STATUS: OK, {access_token: ***, refresh_token: '', expires_at: 1787918657, ...}
bugs.root_cause: li-auth.sh OAuth exchange didn't store the refresh_token (it may not be returned by LinkedIn API unless the app has the right scopes, or li-auth.sh doesn't capture it).
bugs.impact: Token expires in ~60 days. Catherine will need to manually reconnect LinkedIn around Aug 29, 2026.
bugs.fix: Check LinkedIn app permissions — add 'r_basicprofile' scope if missing. Update li-auth.sh to capture and store refresh_token. Implement auto-refresh logic in GATE1 check.

bugs.title: brain-push.sh git sync was broken (482 diverged commits)
bugs.description: brain-push.sh was silently failing on every push due to diverged git history. 482 orphaned commits accumulated. Workspace changes (new files, memory, LinkedIn profiles) were not persisted to git. On pod restart, git reset --hard restored the old state — wiping all recent agent work.
bugs.root_cause: git push was failing due to diverged remote. Script had no error handling and kept creating unreachable commits.
bugs.fix_applied: Added fetch→rebase -X ours→push HEAD:master pattern to brain-push.sh. All diverged history resolved and workspace fully synced to remote.
bugs.impact: Catherine's strategy.md, content-calendar.md, and other files were repeatedly wiped. This is the root cause of BUG-003 and partly BUG-002.

bugs.title: Repeated LinkedIn auth requests — TELEGRAM_USER_ID not exported to subprocess
bugs.description: Agent keeps asking users to re-authorize LinkedIn on every session or even mid-session. The root cause: TELEGRAM_USER_ID was not exported before calling li-auth.sh, so the variable may not be available to the subprocess. Additionally, if $TELEGRAM_USER_ID expands to empty string in the bash context when the script is invoked, the token gets saved under the empty-string key '' instead of the actual UID. Every subsequent GATE1 check then finds no token for the real UID and triggers OAuth again.
evidence: User report: 'agent keeps asking again and again for user to authorize linkedin'
User report: 'just my linkedin got saved properly' (owner UID 264468965 works, others do not)
BUG-002 root cause was identical pattern for li-post.sh — same issue in li-auth.sh was not fixed at the same time
bugs.root_cause: li-auth.sh and li-post.sh both took TELEGRAM_USER_ID only from positional arg ($2 / $1). When the calling bash shell had the variable set but not exported, child processes could not inherit it. If the variable expansion resulted in an empty string, the token was silently saved under key '' and never found on future GATE1 checks.
bugs.fix_applied: 1) li-auth.sh and li-post.sh now use '${arg:-$TELEGRAM_USER_ID}' — arg takes precedence, env var is fallback. Both exit with a clear error if UID is empty. 2) linkedin-publish/SKILL.md Exchange Code and Refresh Token sections now include 'export TELEGRAM_USER_ID' before calling the scripts. 3) AGENTS.md GATE1 check now includes 'export TELEGRAM_USER_ID'. 4) li-auth.sh writes a non-sensitive .linkedin-linked status file to the workspace after successful exchange — this survives pod restarts via brain-push and allows GATE1 to detect 'token store wiped' vs 'never linked'.
bugs.impact: Users had to re-authorize LinkedIn on every session or multiple times per session. Tokens were being saved under empty key and never found.
